title: Estate-wide RBAC grants
description: Role assignments that exceed a single repo's resource-group scope, applied idempotently by scripts/apply-role-grants.ps1 during onboarding. Two cases: 'subscription' scope (e.g. read access for a user-assigned identity), and 'resource' scope on a resource OUTSIDE the identity's own repo RG (e.g. the claude-runner-test broker needing rights on an Azure Arc machine in rg-homelab) — which the repo's own SP cannot assign because it is Owner of only its RG. Repo-scoped RBAC on a resource INSIDE the repo's RG still belongs in that repo's own Bicep instead.

comment.description: Why this grant exists — required reading for whoever reviews changes here.

identityName.description: Name of the user-assigned managed identity receiving the roles.

scope.description: Assignment scope. 'subscription' = the whole subscription. 'resource' = a single resource named by scopeResourceId (for cross-RG grants the owning repo's SP cannot make itself). Anything inside the identity's own repo RG belongs in that repo's Bicep.

scopeResourceId.description: Full ARM resource ID to scope the assignment to. Required when scope is 'resource'; ignored for 'subscription'.

roles.description: Built-in role names to assign (e.g. Reader, Log Analytics Reader).
